mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent within a single quarter, it signals a structural labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE exceeds 4.0% for three consecutive months over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and revolving credit indicates a synchronized unwinding of credit-fueled demand, triggering a demand shock that reduces corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During high-inflation environments, supply constraints captured by industrial production become the primary driver of equity risk premiums; a decline in industrial output amplifies margin compression and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not lead to negative S&P 500 returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 over a 3-year rolling test, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, mechanically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no positive correlation with 60-day S&P 500 forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5 percent, the cost of capital for leveraged firms rises sharply, triggering corporate deleveraging cycles that suppress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not systematically negative when FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5% for two consecutive months over a 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market frictions and wage-push inflation, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer revolving credit reflects increasing household leverage and consumption demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS changes show no predictive power for 5-day S&P 500 forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window.
